title: euphorbia cuneata subsp. cretacea
intro: Euphorbia cuneata subsp. cretacea, also known as the crested spurge or pencil tree, is a subspecies of succulent plant native to Madagascar. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. Euphorbia cuneata subsp. cretacea can be prepared into herbal remedies like decoctions, infusions, and poultices, which are used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
